title: The One with the Girl Who Hits Joey

jokes.text: Listen, we had a good run. What was it? Four, five months? That's more than most people have in a lifetime. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ross is angrily confronting Chandler about seeing him with Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats their friendship like a casual relationship and dramatically exits
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor - only Chandler would handle confrontation this way. Great structure with the false romantic breakup language applied to friendship.

jokes.text: I am going on the lam.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what he's doing as he tries to escape
jokes.punchline: Chandler uses dramatic criminal terminology for avoiding an awkward conversation
jokes.explanation: Solid Chandler line with good economy. The criminal language is amusing but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Hey, Ross. What's up, bro?
jokes.setup: Ross is furious and Chandler was just trying to escape
jokes.punchline: Chandler attempts casual friendliness in the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Chandler's inappropriate social responses, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: What's going on? I think Ross knows about me and Monica.
jokes.setup: Ross is clearly furious and confrontational
jokes.punchline: Chandler states the incredibly obvious with casual uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor - the situation is blindingly obvious but Chandler treats it as a mild suspicion.

jokes.text: Dude, he's right there.
jokes.setup: Chandler loudly announces Ross knows about him and Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the obvious tactical error in a deadpan way
jokes.explanation: Good economy and timing, Joey as the voice of basic common sense. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: You guys want to probably get some hugs in too. Big news!
jokes.setup: Emotional moment where Monica and Chandler declare love while Ross processes
jokes.punchline: Joey treats the dramatic revelation like a casual announcement worthy of celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely missing the emotional weight and treating it like good news to share. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: I'm just talking here. He's the one doing your sister.
jokes.setup: Ross gets angry at Joey after Joey suggests letting the lovebirds get back to business
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the logical flaw in Ross being mad at him specifically
jokes.explanation: Joey's blunt logic cuts through the situation perfectly. Good structure and economy, very quotable.

jokes.text: You know what I just realized? If you guys have kids / We're having kids? / I call godfather!
jokes.setup: Joey starts to make a realization about Monica and Chandler's future
jokes.punchline: Monica panics at the mention of kids while Joey immediately claims godfather rights
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from hypothetical to Monica's panic to Joey's immediate self-interest. Shows each character clearly.

jokes.text: Shouldn't her brother be godfather? / Sure. If you cared enough to call it first.
jokes.setup: Chandler logically suggests Ross should be godfather as the brother
jokes.punchline: Joey treats godfather selection like calling shotgun
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and Joey logic. The 'calling it first' concept applied to sacred family roles is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: You're a few steps ahead of us. / Big zero gravity moon steps.
jokes.setup: Monica points out Joey is getting ahead of himself
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates with an absurd space metaphor for how far ahead Joey is
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of Monica's metaphor. The zero gravity moon steps is genuinely original and visual.

jokes.text: She is so cute. You could fit her in your pocket.
jokes.setup: Rachel comments on Katie's cuteness
jokes.punchline: The extreme smallness observation
jokes.explanation: Basic size humor, not particularly original or well-crafted. Sets up the next joke though.

jokes.text: She could fit in that little pocket inside the pocket.
jokes.setup: Rachel says Katie could fit in your pocket
jokes.punchline: Phoebe escalates to an even smaller space - the tiny inner pocket
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the concept more specific and visual. The tiny inner pocket is a nice detail.

jokes.text: But what? / She keeps punching me.
jokes.setup: Joey seems to have reservations about the adorable tiny Katie
jokes.punchline: The unexpected problem is physical violence from someone described as pocket-sized
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and economy. The contrast between 'cute as a pocket' and 'punching me' is perfect. Sets up the episode's B-story beautifully.

jokes.text: She's hurting me. / I know what you need. You need a bodyguard. / What is Ben doing after preschool?
jokes.setup: Joey's genuine distress about being hurt by tiny Katie
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggests hiring Ross's preschool-aged son as Joey's bodyguard
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation. The idea that a preschooler could protect Joey from a tiny woman is genuinely creative and funny.

jokes.text: Joey is having a problem. A little girl is beating him up.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains Joey's situation to Monica and Phoebe
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact delivery of an absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good economy in summarizing the absurd situation. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: [Reaction beat - Monica's sympathetic response interrupted by Katie punching Joey]
jokes.setup: Monica begins offering Joey sympathy for his difficult situation
jokes.punchline: Katie immediately proves the point by punching Joey mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for physical comedy. The interruption of sympathy with proof of the problem is well-structured.

jokes.text: Salt, so your life always has flavor. Bread, so you never go hungry. And a scented candle for the bathroom. Because, well, you know.
jokes.setup: Phoebe brings housewarming gifts with traditional meanings
jokes.punchline: The progression from meaningful traditions to practical bathroom reality
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe - mixing spiritual tradition with practical bathroom needs. Good escalation from poetic to crude.

jokes.text: Mr. President.
jokes.setup: Steve introduces himself as president of the tenants' committee
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treats the building committee president with presidential reverence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - taking things literally and seriously when others wouldn't. Great economy.

jokes.text: Well, the guy's worked here for 25 years. / Yes, but I've lived here for 25 minutes.
jokes.setup: Steve emphasizes Howard's long tenure to justify the contribution
jokes.punchline: Ross contrasts it with his extremely short tenure using the same time format
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and economy. The parallel construction of '25 years' vs '25 minutes' is perfectly crafted and very quotable.

jokes.text: I mean, I don't know Howard. / Howard's the handyman. / Yes, but to me, he's just 'man.'
jokes.setup: Ross explains he doesn't know Howard, Steve clarifies Howard's role
jokes.punchline: Ross reduces Howard to generic humanity since he lacks the personal connection
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'handyman' becoming just 'man.' Shows Ross's analytical mind applied to social situations.

jokes.text: Can you believe that guy? / I really like his glasses.
jokes.setup: Ross expects Phoebe to agree about Steve being unreasonable
jokes.punchline: Phoebe completely ignores the conflict to comment on Steve's fashion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response - completely missing the social tension and focusing on something random. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: A little kid spit on my knee. And told me to wipe it off with my $100 bill.
jokes.setup: Ross explains how the building residents hate him
jokes.punchline: Even children are hostile and making specific references to his cheapness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the building's hostility. The specificity of the child's insult is funny and shows how the story spread.

jokes.text: You really should wipe it off with something.
jokes.setup: Ross tells the story of the kid spitting on him and the insult
jokes.punchline: Phoebe ignores the social implications and focuses on hygiene practicality
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Phoebe moment - missing the emotional point entirely and focusing on practical matters. Great economy.

jokes.text: If you want them to like you, why don't you just pay the $100? / The party will cost you more. / That doesn't matter! It's my principles!
jokes.setup: Phoebe logically suggests the simple solution and points out the cost inefficiency
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals this isn't actually about money but about being right
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ross's stubbornness and Phoebe's practical logic. Exposes Ross's real motivation.

jokes.text: I thought it was about your neighbors liking you. / They'll like me once they come to my awesome party.
jokes.setup: Phoebe points out the contradiction in Ross's stated goal
jokes.punchline: Ross doubles down with misplaced confidence in his party-throwing abilities
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's delusional confidence. The word 'awesome' is particularly funny given what we know about Ross and parties.

jokes.text: And that crazy party animal will be your brother-in-law.
jokes.setup: Ross leaves talking about nametags for his party
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically refers to Ross as a 'crazy party animal'
jokes.explanation: Straightforward sarcasm playing off Ross's square personality. Not particularly original but functional.

jokes.text: I see two Monicas. My friend who lives across the hall and wants a lot of babies. And the new Monica who I started to date. Now, who is to say what she wants?
jokes.setup: Rachel points out Monica obviously wants marriage and babies
jokes.punchline: Chandler's delusional compartmentalization of the same person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler commitment phobia - creating elaborate mental gymnastics to avoid obvious reality. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I'm completely different from Richard. He's an eye doctor, and I don't wear glasses.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to convince himself he's different from Monica's ex
jokes.punchline: Completely superficial and illogical reasoning for being different
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler logic - completely missing the actual issue (age/commitment readiness) and focusing on irrelevant details.

jokes.text: Not unless different means 'the same.'
jokes.setup: Rachel confirms Chandler's delusion about being different from Richard
jokes.punchline: Dry logic pointing out the contradiction in terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and wordplay. The logical absurdity is cleanly expressed and quotable.

jokes.text: You know, bread-stick fangs are always funny. / No, you make them funny. You're the funny one!
jokes.setup: Joey makes a casual observation about breadstick humor
jokes.punchline: Katie's over-the-top flattery followed by the punch we know is coming
jokes.explanation: Sets up the callback to Katie's violent affection. The breadstick fangs observation is mildly funny but mainly functional.

jokes.text: ...it feels like I'm being hit with a very tiny but a very real bat!
jokes.setup: Joey tries to explain Katie's punches feel harder than they look
jokes.punchline: The specific and vivid metaphor of a 'very tiny but very real bat'
jokes.explanation: Great Joey metaphor - specific, visual, and captures the absurdity perfectly. The 'very tiny but very real' construction is particularly good.

jokes.text: Are you making fun of my size? Don't make fun of me because of my size!
jokes.setup: Joey gently tries to address the punching issue
jokes.punchline: Katie completely misinterprets and becomes more aggressive about the wrong thing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - she's completely missing the point and getting defensive about something Joey didn't even say.

jokes.text: Yeah, here, somewhere else. You know, wherever.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he could stay in bed with her forever
jokes.punchline: Chandler's non-committal response that completely defeats the romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler commitment avoidance - turning a romantic moment into geographical uncertainty. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: Just hanging out. Having fun with the girl that I'm seeing casually.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what he's doing after his weird response
jokes.punchline: Chandler awkwardly over-emphasizes the casual nature to Monica herself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Chandler making things worse by stating the obvious casual framing directly to Monica.

jokes.text: Yes! You want babies! You have baby fever!
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he thinks she wants all that stuff
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked over-agreement with medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler's panic escalating. 'Baby fever' is a funny clinical term for a biological drive.

jokes.text: Why don't we turn the heat down on this pressure cooker!
jokes.setup: The argument is escalating between him and Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler uses cooking metaphors to suggest de-escalation
jokes.explanation: Decent Chandler attempt at diplomacy with an extended cooking metaphor. Somewhat forced but in character.

jokes.text: And that the only baby here is you? Did you know I can't even look at you now?
jokes.setup: Monica is listing things Chandler doesn't know
jokes.punchline: The 'only baby here is you' insult followed by the relationship-ending declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the baby insult turning back on Chandler. The escalation to relationship damage is effective.

jokes.text: I did not know that.
jokes.setup: Monica's dramatic declaration that she can't look at him
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact response treating it like informational exchange
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - completely deflating the drama with literal interpretation. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Who? Two divorces and Joey?
jokes.setup: Chandler says he talked to 'these guys' about relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Monica succinctly summarizes why that's terrible advice sourcing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - Monica destroys Chandler's advisors with just their relationship track records. Highly quotable and brutally accurate.

jokes.text: She's right, you know? / Yeah, but still, cheap shot!
jokes.setup: Monica's brutal assessment of Ross and Joey as relationship advisors
jokes.punchline: Ross agrees she's right while Joey protests the accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Ross's self-awareness vs Joey's wounded pride. Nice follow-up to Monica's burn.

jokes.text: Or you would have seen a Chandler-shaped hole in that door.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what he would have done if she'd said yes to his proposal
jokes.punchline: Chandler describes his escape plan in cartoon physics terms
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy reference to cartoon escapes. Very Chandler and highly quotable. The specificity of 'Chandler-shaped' is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm just the exotic, generous stranger. That's always fun to be.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains why she contributed money when Ross didn't
jokes.punchline: Phoebe enjoys her role as the mysterious benefactor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - she genuinely enjoys being perceived as mysterious and generous. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Okay, my bad.
jokes.setup: The building residents suggest Phoebe and Ross should switch apartments because they like her better
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual acknowledgment that she accidentally made Ross look worse
jokes.explanation: Great economy - Phoebe's casual admission that she completely undermined Ross. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Listen, is it obvious I'm wearing six sweaters? / But it's not obvious why.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if his padding is obvious
jokes.punchline: Chandler confirms it's obvious but mysterious, setting up the explanation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the physical comedy. The 'obvious but mysterious' concept is funny and sets up Joey's explanation well.

jokes.text: ...when I take away the Joey love?
jokes.setup: Joey explains he's worried about how hard Katie will hit him when he breaks up with her
jokes.punchline: Joey's ridiculous terminology for his romantic affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - referring to himself in third person with grandiose terminology. 'Joey love' is very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, right. I forgot that you call it that.
jokes.setup: Joey uses his 'Joey love' terminology
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan acknowledgment of Joey's ridiculous self-branding
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response. Chandler's resigned familiarity with Joey's terminology is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: ...I think you're the one that is too much.
jokes.setup: Katie enthusiastically says Joey's friends are 'too much'
jokes.punchline: Rachel diplomatically but firmly turns it back on Katie
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation. Rachel standing up to Katie is satisfying but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: [Katie kicks Rachel - physical comedy beat]
jokes.setup: Rachel confronts Katie about being 'too much'
jokes.punchline: Katie immediately proves Rachel's point by attacking her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback. Katie immediately proving Rachel's point through violence is well-structured physical comedy.

jokes.text: Do something or I'll walk out that door right now. / Are you going to?
jokes.setup: Rachel gives Joey an ultimatum to defend her
jokes.punchline: Joey's response suggests he might prefer she leave
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Joey completely subverts the ultimatum dynamic. Excellent economy and character-specific cowardice.

jokes.text: But you came off as the bad guy. / I told it wrong.
jokes.setup: Phoebe was trying to get Ross sympathy by telling the Emily story
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admits she somehow made Ross look worse while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - her innocent attempts to help backfiring. Her simple admission is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: 3B. / You got your free food. You ruined everyone's fun. Isn't it time you went home? / Go back to 3B, 3B.
jokes.setup: Someone cut Howard's cake and Steve confronts Ross
jokes.punchline: The building residents turn on Ross with apartment number chanting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the building's hostility. The apartment number becoming a chant is a nice touch showing how united they are against Ross.

jokes.text: He raised his own hand when I asked, 'Who likes Ross?' And he's wearing two nametags.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is trying to defend Ross to the hostile crowd
jokes.punchline: She accidentally provides more evidence of Ross's social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Great callback and perfect Phoebe - trying to help but making things worse. The two nametags detail is perfectly sad and funny.

jokes.text: Kurt? I thought, abrasive drunk. Lola? Mind-numbingly stupid. You guys. Gold digger, cradle-robbing perv.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to explain that first impressions can be wrong
jokes.punchline: She lists increasingly harsh first impressions of the people she's trying to win over
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment - completely undermining her own argument by being brutally honest. The escalation from 'abrasive drunk' to 'cradle-robbing perv' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Obviously, I didn't think they would throw things.
jokes.setup: The crowd has clearly reacted badly to Phoebe's insults
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent surprise at the predictable negative reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe naivety - she genuinely didn't expect people to be offended by brutal honesty. Great understatement.

jokes.text: I thought if I kept insulting them you'd defend them. Then you'd look like the hero.
jokes.setup: Ross asks why she kept insulting people
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals her backwards but logical strategy
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe logic - completely backwards but somehow makes sense in her world. The strategy is actually clever but poorly executed.

jokes.text: What a bad idea. / I can't not look at it.
jokes.setup: Chandler proposes to Monica in the coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Everyone acknowledges it's terrible but can't look away
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat capturing the crowd's horrified fascination. The double negative in Ross's line is nicely awkward.

jokes.text: The best reason is pregnancy.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if sorry is the best reason to get married
jokes.punchline: Monica matter-of-factly states what she considers the number one reason
jokes.explanation: Great economy and Monica's practical approach to marriage. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Sorry is fourth, behind being ready and actually wanting to get married.
jokes.setup: Monica has established pregnancy as the best reason for marriage
jokes.punchline: She has a complete ranking system with sorry being quite low
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - she has analyzed and ranked marriage motivations. The systematic approach to romance is very her and quite funny.

jokes.text: I'm kind of wishing everyone wasn't here now.
jokes.setup: The public proposal is going badly
jokes.punchline: Chandler's massive understatement about the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler understatement - 'kind of wishing' during a complete public humiliation. Great character moment.

jokes.text: How long is this going to go on? / I think the length of teasing is directly related to how insane you were. So a long time.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks how long the friends will tease him about the proposal
jokes.punchline: Joey provides a mathematical formula based on Chandler's level of insanity
jokes.explanation: Great structure and Joey logic - creating a scientific principle for teasing duration. The 'So a long time' conclusion is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: This is fun! / Remember that 'we were on a break' thing? / I'm sorry. Will you marry me?
jokes.setup: The teasing continues with Rachel enjoying herself
jokes.punchline: Rachel brings up Ross's classic line, then Chandler immediately proposes to deflect
jokes.explanation: Good callback to a classic Friends running gag, with Chandler's panic proposal as deflection being perfectly in character.

tmdb_overview: Joey dates Katie, a physically aggressive woman; Ross offends the building's maintenance staff.
